title: Japanese Nationalism in NGE
wikitext:
[]: Japanese Nationalism in NGE             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jan 25, 2013 3:39 pm Split from here -- http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/13257/Anime-that-Ended-Well/ Alaska Slim wrote: I wouldn't call it an overstatement:

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Fri Jan 25, 2013 8:11 pm I do like this line of conversation, but I believe it deserves its own thread. Not sure about a title, though... Alaska Slim wrote: And then there's the Star Trek comments .

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Fri Jan 25, 2013 8:27 pm EvangelionFan wrote: Although it is unclear if Anno is referring to the original series or the more prominent films and spin-offs I would also appreciate it if you offered an elaboration as to how Star Trek 's futurist ideology of a United Earth or a 'Federation' is similar to Gunbuster 's subtext of a dominant and pro-Imperial Japan.

[]: Re: Not so much. B/             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sat Jan 26, 2013 5:32 am Bagheera wrote: (and note that America was not "manhandled" at any point in the show;

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jan 26, 2013 10:47 am Oh man, this ain't even worth it. But you're wrong about Unit 04; its demise was the result of an accident, not sabotage. They speculate about sabotage while discussing it, but the possibility is ultimately rejected (and if Anno wanted us to think it was sabotage he would have told us, just as he did with the Jet Alone incident).

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Sat Jan 26, 2013 10:32 pm didn't he tell us it was sabotage with the whole "it was an accident, or was it" line?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jan 26, 2013 10:49 pm sum_exsanguis wrote: didn't he tell us it was sabotage with the whole "it was an accident, or was it" line?

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Sat Jan 26, 2013 11:13 pm 1. Because we can start believing her as a character when? 2. You are right it didn't explode, but it was being tested on and the AT field broke down. Does Nerv/Seele make mistakes at that level? Isn't it convenient that it leads to the whole Eva 03 becomes an angel? The fact of the matter is just because it didn't explode doesn't mean any testing on it could not of been sabotaged. Kaji is the one voice of truth in the show (in my opinion). I stick with him.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jan 26, 2013 11:59 pm sum_exsanguis wrote: 1. Because we can start believing her as a character when?

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Sun Jan 27, 2013 12:20 am But we cannot trust her when it comes to the top secret stuff. I may be mistaken, but doesn't she tell Misato the giant is Adam and not Lillith? Also, what has Nerv actually messed up? Unit 00 and the activation test, but that is not on the same level as this. What else is there?

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 27, 2013 12:28 am sum_exsanguis wrote: But we cannot trust her when it comes to the top secret stuff. I may be mistaken, but doesn't she tell Misato the giant is Adam and not Lillith?

[]:             Quote Post by sum_exsanguis » Sun Jan 27, 2013 11:03 am [s:33p3d8ju]He is right we do need to go back to the topic of this thread...[/s:33p3d8ju]I just don't understand how something you make happen is egg on Nerv/Seele's face. They made 2nd impact happen and they wanted unit 01 to be awakened. I'm not saying they just go out and win, but they usually have a good grasp on controllable issues

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sun Jan 27, 2013 8:15 pm sum_exsanguis wrote: [s]He is right we do need to go back to the topic of this thread...[/s]I just don't understand how something you make happen is egg on Nerv/Seele's face. They made 2nd impact happen and they wanted unit 01 to be awakened. I'm not saying they just go out and win, but they usually have a good grasp on controllable issues

[]:             Quote Post by Clover » Sun Jan 27, 2013 10:28 pm Japan's jabs at America are always funny. It's literally all they can do.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jan 28, 2013 9:08 am The "geez, those Americans!" sentiment expressed in some parts of EVA does not necessarily imply rampant nationalism. I know for sure that the reactions among the German fanbase to those little stabs at America was "Nod, nod" as evidenced by rather entertaining youtube comments and episode sumaries. You see, it is also a very, very common sentiment in Europe and other parts of the world - it's not that we think we're better than you, many of us just don't like the foreighn policies of your government and feel like some of your politicians act like they own all of the planet, that they don't cooperate with the rest of the world community for things like preserving the environment, but still want to be the leaders. I'm not arguing wether it's true or not, I'm just saying that this sentiment exists , that it's not exclusive to crazy nationalists, and that certainly not all, but some Americans really overestimate their popularity in the world and act like everyone who just doesn't like them is immediately opposed to truth and freedom. Yeah, you guys practically invented it, but that was centuries ago . You can see that same thing reflected in things like british Sci-Fi dramas and amongst a bunch of particularly snarky German teenagers, you will probably be called a gullible, manipulated sheep if you don't disagree with America's foreign policies... and the word "nationalist" is almost treated like an insult over here... - again, not saying it's the truth or that I agree, but that it's a common sentiment. This is also why Obama is so popular in Europe, in case that still puzzles any of you - Because he noticeably toned down the unilateralism thing, so from our PoV, he's easier to deal with. (admittedly, we're not the ones who have to live under him so it shouldn't be any of our business, but the sad thing is, if the American president is good to tolerable, then he really has nothing to do with us, but if he sux at his job, Europe usually feels it, too. Basically, we don't like being stepped on or treated like we can't solve our own problems because or great-gradparents couldn't, and I can't blame the Japanese for feeling the same way, everyone involved is dead or ancient, we don't like them either and it's just no longer the same society, since the sixties,the post-war generation has 180-fied it to the point of overcompensation, I love the Rebuild dub team for rendering a certain line of Kensuke's as "Die Amis haben wohl schiss gekriegt!" because that's exactly how your average German school boy would render it....

[]:             Quote Post by Anubis » Mon Jan 28, 2013 2:54 pm Actually, Obama is only popular in Western Europe; you get to countries like Poland, Ukraine, and Hungary you would find that they are very cold towards Obama compared to Bush. As for Japan's anti-Americanism, Japan needs to remember that the rest of Asia is not too fond of them due to their actions during WW2. Any Asian anger towards the US is mostly due to Vietnam (which does not hold a grudge) and American protection of Japan for its past crimes. Many Asian countries feel that Japan got off too lightly in their defeat.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Mon Jan 28, 2013 3:27 pm Anubis wrote: Ukraine, and Hungary .

[]:             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Mon Jan 28, 2013 6:04 pm Kendrix wrote: Yeah, you guys practically invented it

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Jan 28, 2013 6:30 pm Anubis wrote: As for Japan's anti-Americanism, Japan needs to remember that the rest of Asia is not too fond of them due to their actions during WW2. Any Asian anger towards the US is mostly due to Vietnam (which does not hold a grudge) and American protection of Japan for its past crimes. Many Asian countries feel that Japan got off too lightly in their defeat.

[]:             Quote Post by Darkwing » Mon Jan 28, 2013 9:15 pm Well it's sort of understandable if they still are upset about the imperial Japanese occupation in Manchuria. They still dig up some of the old viral warfare projects to this day. Literally, they hit capsules and the like digging house foundations, and sometimes get outbreaks of the disease.
